comment.body:
I don’t use technical indicators derived from
Price action. Moving averages / macd / stochastics. They are just looking at the past and that’s not a predictor of the future. Anything backward looking is not relevant

comment.body: I wonder what’s you guys’s take on indicators? do they give the real info that changes the price movement? (For me no, because price movements are a lot depends on positioning ) but all those MACD, TTM squeeze, RSI look pretty good when they show an overbought/oversold, or the”bullish cross” etc.

I reference is the McClellan oscillator which is not a measure of price but of market breadth

comment.body: Yes ttm squeeze is John carters indicator. That one has some price volatility compared with moving averages to time trades. Don’t get me wrong a lot of people are successful using moving averages to tactically enter and exit trades. It does help ensure discipline against a system. And discipline is important in staying consistent with a process.

comment.body:
By the way, I tell you a "secret" how can you spare hundreds if not thousands of dollars a year...

If you buy Graham - Intelligent Investor, Natenberg - Options Volatility and Pricing, Bennett - Trading Volatility and Taleb - Dynamic Hedging (very advanced), you spent 185-230 USD only once, (but note that these books are avaible online for free in PDF format)

If you are an IBKR user, real time options + futures data for equities is approximately around $600/year

Lets say you spend $97/month for SG pro, or $224/month for OD promax (including my -10% discount)

It is $3543 in a year, assuming you bought the books and didn't used the discount.
